editableGoldMemories.label: Thoughts/Goals/Long_Term
editableGoldMemories.value: Aims to apply environmental engineering knowledge to solve water shortage and chemical pollution issues in Iran
editableGoldMemories.reasoning: Stated as future intent rather than current attribute,can be categorized as the user's long-term goal

evidence.text:
My main purpose in studying Environmental Engineering is to develop deep knowledge in environmental applications, especially for removing chemical pollutants. This lets me get what was always missing in my Bachelor’s degree and first master’s degree which was more relating to science and the nature of reactions and elements not environmental issues which originate from reactions. Environmental issues have been discriminated in my country during the years and there is any practical approach to remove or decrease of chemical pollutant in labs, pharmaceutical companies and chemical industries. The existence of toxic components in pharmaceutical wastes causes worries in my home country. Entrance of these toxic components of pharmaceutical industry, especially anticancer drugs in to the ecosystem throw wastes, threat human health, leaving creatures and even plants. Therefore, purification of pharmaceutical wastes in industry is highly vital. 
One big problem with chemical pollutants is that there is not any Environmentalist in Pharmaceutical companies and industries who can use observations and small pilot studies to describe how these companies can get rid of harmful substances and wastes. While to survive, we need to incorporate science and technology as complementary to traditional ways of studying environmental issues, and I believe that as a chemist who has a years of research experience in medicinal chemistry should have a solid knowledge of environment and try to reduce releasing detrimental substances to the environment. I am confident that through the advantages lying in Environmental Engineering capabilities, I can have the scientific inputs and supports that I need to validate both my research and professional work. So I can have a promotion prospect and move up the career lather.

Living my whole life in a country where struggling with severe environmental problems, I realized that my educational and research background cannot necessarily give me robust analytical tools and methods for evaluating and making intelligent decisions in dealing with environmental problems. Though this is the area where I am interested: integrating my acquired Environmental Engineering knowledge into my previous academic background and use my knowledge to remove toxic wastes from water resources or try to decrease chemical wastes. 

I would like to mention that I have decided to take this two-year opportunity to improve my knowledge to some extent on the above-mentioned topics.

editableGoldMemories.label: Preferences/Education
editableGoldMemories.value: Prefers Canadian education for environmental engineering due to practical focus and multicultural environment

editableNewDialogue.text: Quick question: if I paste a paragraph, can you help me make it sound more formal without changing the meaning?

Yes. Paste the paragraph and tell me the intended audience (e.g., university, embassy, employer) and the tone (formal, confident, neutral). I’ll keep the meaning and improve clarity, grammar, and flow.
Also, should I keep long sentences, or split them up?

Usually split them. As a rule of thumb: 1 idea per sentence, and aim for 15–25 words when possible. If a sentence has multiple clauses or several “and/but/which,” it’s a good candidate to break into two.
I sometimes repeat the same point in different words. Is that bad?

In formal writing, repetition can look unfocused. Keep one strong version of the point, then add either (a) a concrete example, or (b) a specific outcome. If two sentences say the same thing, delete or merge.
Unrelated: I read that the Voyager probes still send signals sometimes. Is that true?

Yes. Voyager 1 and 2 are still communicating with Earth, though their instruments are being turned off over time to conserve power. Their signals are extremely faint and received by NASA’s Deep Space Network.
Ok, I’ll paste the paragraph next. Should I include headings or just plain text?

Headings help if you’re answering multiple questions (e.g., “Motivation,” “Academic Preparation,” “Future Plans”). If it’s one short response, plain text is fine. Either way, keep formatting consistent.

For the next question, they asked me to explain ties to my home country. I’m thinking to mention that my parents are getting older, so after the program I need to go back and be close to them while I continue my work in Iran.

Yes—framing it around family responsibilities can be a strong, credible tie. If you want, I can rewrite that point in a formal tone and connect it to your career plan in Iran without sounding repetitive.

I should correct something: I didn’t choose Canada because it’s multicultural or for the lifestyle. My decision is mainly academic and practical. I need a program that matches my background in chemistry and lets me learn real methods for water and wastewater treatment, especially removing chemical pollutants from industrial and pharmaceutical wastes.

Got it. So your preference isn’t based on Canada’s multicultural environment; it’s because you want a practical Environmental Engineering curriculum—especially water/wastewater treatment and removing chemical pollutants from pharmaceutical and chemical industry waste—so you can apply it professionally back home.
